user.title: Set up WANPulse — Internet Monitor
user.description:
WANPulse monitors your internet connection by periodically probing external servers. It measures latency, packet loss, jitter and detects outages.

**Probe targets** — enter one target per line in the format:
`host, label, method`

- **host** — IP address or hostname to probe (e.g. `1.1.1.1`, `google.com`)
- **label** — a friendly name shown in Home Assistant (e.g. `Cloudflare`)
- **method** — probe type: `tcp` (default, recommended), `http`, or `dns`

**Examples:**
- `1.1.1.1, Cloudflare DNS, tcp` — tests TCP connection to Cloudflare
- `https://www.google.com, Google, http` — tests HTTP response from Google
- `www.google.com, Google DNS, dns` — tests DNS resolution for Google

data_description.targets: One target per line in the format: host, label, method. The default targets (Cloudflare and Google DNS) are a good starting point for most users.
data_description.scan_interval: How often WANPulse checks your internet (10–3600 seconds). Default 60s is a good balance between accuracy and resource usage.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure WANPulse targets
reconfigure.description:
Edit your probe targets. One target per line: `host, label, method`

- **TCP** (recommended) — works with any IP or hostname, tests connection to port 443
- **HTTP** — provide a full URL, tests HTTP HEAD response
- **DNS** — provide a hostname, tests DNS resolution time

data_description.targets: One target per line: host, label, method. You can add up to 10 targets.

error.no_targets: At least one target is required. Enter a host like: 1.1.1.1, Cloudflare, tcp
error.too_many_targets: Maximum {max_targets} targets allowed. Remove some targets and try again.
error.invalid_host: One or more targets have an invalid or empty host field.
error.invalid_host_format: Invalid host format. Use an IP address (e.g. 1.1.1.1), hostname (e.g. google.com), or URL (e.g. https://google.com) depending on probe method.
error.invalid_method: Invalid probe method. Supported methods: tcp, http, dns.
error.all_targets_unreachable: None of the targets could be reached. Check your internet connection and target addresses. Unreachable: {unreachable}

abort.already_configured: WANPulse is already configured. You can modify targets via Reconfigure or change settings via Configure.
abort.reconfigure_successful: Targets updated successfully. WANPulse will now monitor the new targets.

init.title: WANPulse — Monitoring Settings
init.description: Fine-tune how WANPulse monitors your internet connection. The defaults work well for most users.

data_description.scan_interval: How often WANPulse checks your connection. Lower = more responsive but more traffic. Recommended: 30–120 seconds.
data_description.timeout: Maximum time to wait for a single probe response. Increase if you have a slow connection. Default: 10 seconds.
data_description.probe_count: Number of probes sent per target each cycle. More probes = more accurate metrics but more traffic. Default: 3.
data_description.failure_threshold: How many consecutive failed cycles before WANPulse declares an outage. Higher = fewer false alarms. Default: 3.

no_valid_targets.title: WANPulse: No valid targets configured
no_valid_targets.description: WANPulse could not start because no valid probe targets are configured. Go to Settings > Devices & Services > WANPulse > three-dot menu > Reconfigure to add at least one valid target.

aggressive_polling.title: WANPulse: Very short polling interval
aggressive_polling.description: The configured scan interval ({scan_interval}s) is very aggressive. This may cause excessive network traffic and CPU usage. Consider increasing it to at least 30 seconds via Settings > Devices & Services > WANPulse > Configure.
